messages.content:
The stimuli for the activation of RAAS system are the following:

Select one or more:

Increase of the arterial blood pressure

Hypoalbuminemia

Increase of Ca2+ ions concentration in blood

Decrease of kidney blood supply

Significant decrease of the arterial blood pressure

Increase of the arterial blood pressure
Decrease of kidney blood supply
Significant decrease of the arterial blood pressure

Norepinephrin (noradrenaline) released from the sympathetic nerve fibers to vascular smooth muscles causes VASODILATION when it binds to the following receptors:

Select one:

Beta 2 adrenergic receptors

Muscarinic cholinergic receptors

Alpha 1 adrenergic receptors

Beta 1 adrenergic receptors
Beta 2 adrenergic receptors

According to the basic law of hemodynamics, arterial blood pressure is equal to the multiplication of:

Select one:

Volume of the circulating blood by total peripheral resistance

Cardiac output by the volume of the circulating blood

Cardiac output by total peripheral resistance

Volume of the circulating blood by blood viscosity
Cardiac output by total peripheral resistance

The FINAL effect of the RAAS system activation is the following:

Select one:

Increase of blood pressure due to increased circulating blood volume

Increase of blood pressure due to systemic vasoconstriction

Constriction of the skin vessels and dilation of the intestinal vessels

Increased diuresis
Increase of blood pressure due to increased circulating blood volume

FAST (reflectory) mechanisms of blood pressure regulation involve regulation of the following factors:

Select one or more:

Cardiac output

Blood viscosity

Volume of the circulating blood

Total peripheral resistance

Cardiac output
Total peripheral resistance

Choose the organs having in the smooth muscles of their vessels predominantly beta 2 adrenergic receptors:

Select one or more:

Intestines

Skin

Heart

Skeletal muscles

Kidney

Skin
Skeletal muscles

Negative chronotropic effect of the VAGUS nerve fibers on the heart is based on the following influence on the conductive system cells:

Select one:

Increase of permeability to K+ ions

Increase of permeability to Ca2+ ions

Decrease of permeability to K+ ions

Increase of permeability to Na+ ions
Increase of permeability to K+ ions

Baroreceptors of the AORTIC ARCH send impulses to the brainstem through:

Select one:

Vagus nerve (X)

Glossopharyngeal nerve (IX)

Facial nerve

Hypoglossal nerve
Glossopharyngeal nerve (IX)

The second neurons of the SYMPATHETIC nerve fibers for the heart regulation are located in:

Select one:

Cardiac intramural ganglia

Ganglion stellatum

Lateral horns of the spinal cord, Th1-Th3

Medulla
Lateral horns of the spinal cord, Th1-Th3

The second neurons of VAGUS nerve fibers for the heart regulation are located in:

Select one:

Medulla

Cardiac intramural ganglia

Lateral horns of the spinal cord, Th1-Th3

Ganglion stellatum
Medulla

Choose all hormones and substances which INCREASE the heart rate and/or force of contraction:

Select one or more:

Angiotensin II

Insulin

Growth gormone

Epinephrin (Adrenalin)

Norepinephrin (Noradrenalin)

Thyroxin

Angiotensin II 
Epinephrine (Adrenaline)
Norepinephrine (Noradrenaline)

Baroreceptors of the CAROTID SINUS send impulses to the brainstem through:

Select one:

Vagus nerve (X)

Facial nerve

Hypoglossal nerve

Glossopharyngeal nerve (IX)
Glossopharyngeal nerve (IX)

Choose all the heart structures innervated by VAGUS nerve:

Select one or more:

Sinus node

Ventricles

Atria

Atrioventricular node

Sinus node
Atria
Atrioventricular node
